name: Optimize gzipped bundle size for core, hooks, and compat

description: Baseline: benchmark harness builds core/hooks/compat, sums gzip bytes of shipped CJS/ESM/UMD artifacts, and runs unit tests.
Tried factoring compat render/hydrate through a shared helper to reduce repeated callback/return logic; compat gzip grew by 36 bytes.
Tried building compat default export from a hooks namespace plus assign() to avoid listing every hook explicitly; bundle got 164 bytes worse, so explicit shorthand exports compress better.
Backported a hooks size optimization: iterate the full hook list in useReducer bailout logic and always return hookState._value. Saved 68 gzip bytes in hooks with tests passing.
Backported a compat Suspense size optimization from main: removed a hydration-only vnode.type mutation on unmount and inlined fallback creation. Saved 70 gzip bytes in compat with tests passing.
Replaced process._rerenderCount property accesses in src/component.js with a local rerenderCount variable. Saved 13 gzip bytes in core with tests passing.
Tried caching component._parentDom in renderComponent; the extra local increased core gzip by 65 bytes, so the direct property access compresses better here.
Simplified core ref cleanup paths in src/diff/index.js by inlining function-ref unmount checks and flattening the vnode.ref unmount condition. Saved 7 more gzip bytes in core with tests passing.
Kept INSERT_VNODE placement state numeric instead of coercing it to boolean in src/diff/children.js. Saved 9 more gzip bytes in core with tests passing.
Tried inlining the isMounting check in src/diff/children.js. It was size-neutral after minification, so I discarded it to avoid churn.
Tried loosening defaultProps undefined checks in src/create-element.js and src/clone-element.js. Benchmark was unchanged, but createElement tests failed for null props semantics, so this is not safe.
